3.happening: Use the input values directly as the nodes — there is no dummy in this approach.
3.why: Instead of a sentinel, we'll handle the head as a special case in Phase 1.

4.happening: Build links: node i points to node i+1, and the last node points to -1 (null).
4.why: The renderer draws one arrow per link; -1 ends the chain.
4.invariant: links describes the arrows between nodes.

5.happening: removed starts all zeros — nothing deleted yet.
5.why: A 1 flags a node so the renderer paints it red.

6.why: It names the arrow rerouted each step so the renderer can flash it.
6.invariant: empty unless a bypass just happened.

7.happening: start marks the head node — index 0, or -1 if the list is empty.
7.why: We may have to move the head itself if it holds val.
7.invariant: start is the current first node.

8.happening: The head node's value ({values[start]}) equals val ({val}) — it must go.
8.why: A leading match can't be bypassed from behind, so we drop the head directly.
8.invariant: start still points at a matching head node.

8.happening: The head no longer matches val (or the list is empty) — Phase 1 is done.
8.why: The front of the list is now clean; the rest is handled by the main loop.
8.invariant: start is a safe head, or -1.

9.happening: Mark the matching head as removed (red).
9.why: This leading node holds val and is being dropped.

10.happening: Move the head forward: start = links[start].
10.why: The old head is discarded; the next node becomes the head.

11.happening: Phase 1 consumed the whole list — nothing remains.
The list still has nodes after Phase 1.
11.why: There is a real head to keep and more nodes to scan.

12.happening: The answer is the empty list.

13.why: There is nothing left to scan.
13.invariant: Done — the whole list was val.

14.happening: Phase 2: current starts at the safe head (start).
14.why: From here we test each node's NEXT, exactly like the dummy approach.
14.invariant: current sits before the next candidate.

15.happening: current has a next node — keep scanning.
current.next is -1 — the scan is finished.

16.happening: Look at the candidate nxt = links[current] — the node after current.
16.why: This is the node we'll delete or keep.

17.happening: The candidate's value ({values[nxt]}) equals val ({val}) — delete it.
The candidate's value ({values[nxt]}) isn't val ({val}) — keep it.

18.happening: Bypass: current's arrow skips the candidate to whatever followed it.
18.why: Not following the matched node deletes it.
18.invariant: The live list is one node shorter.

19.happening: Detach the removed node's arrow (set it to -1).
19.why: It shouldn't point back into the live list.
19.invariant: The removed node links to nothing.

20.why: So you can see which node was skipped.

21.happening: Record current's rerouted arrow so it flashes.

23.happening: Keep the candidate: advance current onto it.
23.why: A survivor — move to the next test.

24.happening: No arrow changed this step, so clear the flash.

25.happening: Start collecting the survivors into result.
25.why: The answer is the values still linked from start.

26.happening: walk starts at start — the (possibly shifted) head.
26.why: The cleaned list begins at the head Phase 1 left us with.

27.happening: walk points at a surviving node — record it.
27.why: Collect every remaining node in order.

27.happening: walk is -1 — the end of the cleaned list.

28.happening: Append this survivor's value ({values[walk]}) to result.
28.why: It wasn't removed, so it belongs in the answer.

29.happening: Follow the arrow to the next surviving node.

30.why: Every val-valued node has been removed, including any at the head.

init.happening: Setting up the node array, links, and the head pointer.
init.why: No dummy here — the head is handled specially in Phase 1.
init.invariant: start on index 0 (or -1 if the list is empty).

loop.happening: A loop header — testing whether to keep scanning.
loop.why: Phase 1 tests the head; Phase 2 tests current.next; the final loop walks survivors.
loop.invariant: The condition decides whether the block runs again.

check.happening: Comparing a node's value to val.
check.why: Only nodes whose value equals val are removed.
check.invariant: A node is about to be dropped or kept.

update.happening: Rewiring arrows and marking removed nodes.
update.why: Deletion is pointer-skipping, not data shifting.
update.invariant: The live chain reflects every deletion so far.

move.happening: Advancing a pointer along the list.
move.why: Shift the head, advance current, or walk the final chain.
move.invariant: The pointer steps one node to the right.

return.happening: Returning the cleaned list of values.
return.why: Every val-valued node has been removed.
return.invariant: result holds only survivors, in original order.
